flavorText: Life clerics preserve and restore vitality with unmatched divine healing.

features.description: When you cast a spell that restores hit points, the creature regains extra hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.
As Channel Divinity, restore hit points among creatures within 30 feet equal to 5 × your cleric level; this can't restore a creature above half its hit point maximum.
When you cast a healing spell on others, you regain hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.
Once on each of your turns when you hit with a weapon attack, deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage (2d8 at 14th level).
When you would roll dice to restore hit points with a spell, use the highest number possible for each die instead.

life-bonus-proficiency.summary: Start better armored and more comfortable standing in the middle of the party you are protecting.
life-bonus-proficiency.description: Heavy armor proficiency lets the Life cleric stay planted at the heart of the group. You can absorb hits, hold position near injured allies, and keep healing flowing without scrambling away from the front.

life-disciple-of-life.summary: Your healing spells become materially stronger, turning every slot into better party stabilization.
life-disciple-of-life.description: Disciple of Life adds 2 + the spell's level to every healing spell you cast. A 1st-level Cure Wounds heals an extra 3 HP; a 5th-level Mass Cure Wounds heals an extra 7 HP per creature. This is what separates the Life cleric from a cleric who occasionally heals.

life-preserve-life.summary: Redistribute a strong burst of emergency healing across the team when attrition is breaking the party line.
life-preserve-life.description: Preserve Life distributes healing equal to 5 × your cleric level among any creatures within 30 feet, split however you choose. No creature can be healed above half its hit point maximum, so it works best when multiple allies are in the danger zone at once. At level 10 that pool is 50 HP — enough to stabilize an entire combat group.

life-blessed-healer.summary: When you heal others, you also keep yourself steadier, helping the healer stay active in the same fight.
life-blessed-healer.description: Every time you cast a healing spell on someone other than yourself, you regain 2 + the spell's level in hit points. This passive loop means the act of supporting the party keeps you online, reducing the pressure to self-heal separately.

life-divine-strike.summary: Even the healing-focused domain keeps a bit of divine radiant pressure in reserve for weapon turns.
life-divine-strike.description: Once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, you add 1d8 radiant damage (2d8 at level 14). It is not the domain's primary identity, but it keeps weapon turns from being wasted.

life-supreme-healing.summary: Your healing magic becomes fully reliable — always roll the maximum possible result on every healing die.
life-supreme-healing.description: Supreme Healing removes the variance from all healing dice. Mass Cure Wounds always gives the full number; Heal always gives its full 70 HP. In dangerous late-game fights where a high-variance roll could mean the difference between a creature standing or falling, this turns every healing slot into a guaranteed outcome.
